stocks.recent_news: Multiple capacity COD announcements in April 2026: NTPC Green Khavda-II 90 MW solar from Apr 25 (group capacity to 89,705 MW); NGEL 87.5 MW Rajasthan solar from Apr 19; NGEL 150 MW from Apr 18; THDC Tehri PSP Unit 4 (250 MW) from Apr 12. Q3 FY26 PAT ~Rs 4,711 Cr. PTC India restructuring underway with NTPC becoming sole promoter per Ministry of Power directive (Jan 23, 2026). Trading at Rs 411.40 on Apr 27.
stocks.sector_context: India power sector enjoying strong tailwinds: hotter-than-usual summer driving record peak demand; AI/data centers/EVs adding ~30 GW over coming years; 900 GW transmission target by 2034; 500 GW renewable target by 2030. JM Financial highlights up to 22% upside in select power names. Strong policy push for renewables and grid expansion.
stocks.peer_comparison: Analysts prefer NTPC and Tata Power amid the demand surge. NTPC trades at a valuation discount vs broader power-gen peers given regulated returns plus growing renewable optionality through NTPC Green. Capacity additions outpacing many peers; defensive earnings profile better than merchant generators (Adani Power, JSW Energy).
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results board meeting May 15, 2026; PAT estimate Rs 5,000-5,600 Cr
FY26 final dividend expected Rs 5-8/share at May 15 board meeting
Continued NTPC Green capacity COD announcements (group capacity nearing 90 GW)
Heatwave-driven peak demand continuing through summer

stocks.recent_news: Q3 FY26 net profit up 8.37% YoY to Rs 4,184.96 Cr; sales up 10.35% to Rs 12,395 Cr. Mwanga Transmission Co. incorporated in Kenya on Apr 21, 2026 for Kenya PPP transmission project (international expansion). Two independent directors (Shiv Tapasya Paswan, Rohit Vaswani) completed tenure on Apr 15, 2026. Interest payment of Rs 416.4 Cr made on Apr 15 - no delays. Trading window closed from Mar 31 ahead of Q4 results. Will relinquish PTC India promoter status as part of governance simplification (NTPC becoming sole promoter).
stocks.sector_context: Direct beneficiary of India's 900 GW transmission target by 2034 and renewable integration buildout. Power Grid holds ~86% control over inter-regional transmission - dominant moat. Stable regulated returns model insulates it from merchant power volatility hurting some generators. Among the highest dividend yields in power sector (~3.4-6%).
stocks.peer_comparison: POWERGRID is effectively a transmission monopoly with regulated equity returns - no direct large-cap listed peer. Compared to other PSU power names, lower beta, steadier earnings, higher dividend yield. Q3 PAT growth of 8.37% solid for a regulated-return business. Some houses rate 'Hold' citing valuation vs growth trade-off.
red_flags: Two Independent Directors completed tenure on Apr 15, 2026 - board composition transition; replacements not yet confirmed
Trading window closed since Mar 31, limiting any insider activity signal pre-Q4
catalysts: Q4 FY26 board meeting May 19, 2026; revenue est Rs 12,000-12,800 Cr, PAT Rs 3,800-4,100 Cr
FY26 final dividend expected Rs 4.50-5.50/share; total FY26 dividend Rs 7.50-8.50
Kenya transmission JV execution provides international growth optionality
Regulated equity growth 8-10% on capex execution
